description: Wave 8: Sable Reach specialists + borderline/almost-right rejects that teach nuance, not just obvious failures.
style_prefix: oil painting, semi-realistic painterly character concept art, muted dusty palette, subtle dark edges, soft upper-left directional lighting, worn and functional aesthetic, lived-in sci-fi, visible material texture and surface grain, visible brushstrokes, full body visible, front-facing standing pose, isolated on plain dark grey background
negative: photorealistic, photograph, photo, stock photo, 3d render, smooth CG, CGI, octane render, unreal engine, anime, cartoon, chibi, bright saturated colors, clean pristine new clothing, glamorous, heroic pose, dynamic action pose, multiple characters, busy background, environmental background, landscape, text, watermark, blurry, low quality, extra limbs, deformed, medieval, fantasy, superhero

subjects.prompt: human woman in her 30s who navigates for a Reach pirate crew, wearing a stolen Compact nav officer jacket with the department piping ripped out and replaced with hand-stitched dark thread, the jacket dyed poorly from steel-blue to muddy dark brown, civilian trousers, mismatched boots, a personal star chart device strapped to her forearm with cable ties, she is the smartest person on the ship and everyone knows it

large human male who feeds a Reach crew from a galley made of salvage, wearing a stained canvas apron over a torn thermal undersuit, improvised oven mitts made from cargo padding, rubber sandals cut from a cargo mat, a kitchen knife in a sheath that used to be a sidearm holster, he cooks with whatever the crew can steal or trade and makes it edible through sheer force of will

small Keth arthropod alien who serves as healer in the Reach using knowledge from the Communion bio-sciences, warm ivory chitin showing stress cracks, compound eyes with a focused expression, wearing scraps of Keth bio-film that is drying out overlaid with human medical supplies strapped on with tape, a salvaged Compact medical kit modified with Keth biological knowledge, the healing methods are alien but they work

young human male in his 20s who is a technical savant living in the Reach because he was expelled from a Compact technical academy, wearing a mix of Compact cadet coveralls with the insignia removed and civilian clothes underneath, surrounded by jury-rigged tech devices clipped and strapped to every surface of his clothing, data cables wound around his forearms like bracelets, his clothes are secondary to the tech — he is a walking repair shop

old human woman in her 70s who has survived in the Sable Reach for fifty years which is almost unheard of, wearing layers of carefully maintained clothing — a patched but clean thermal base, a leather vest that has been repaired so many times it is more thread than leather, a shawl that might once have been a flag, comfortable adapted boots, no weapons visible but a small knife somewhere, she survives on accumulated knowledge and relationships not violence

wiry human woman in her 30s with nervous energy who handles communications for a Reach crew, wearing a Compact signals department uniform shirt faded from dark blue to pale grey with the patches removed, civilian trousers with extra pockets sewn in for comms parts, headphones permanently around her neck modified with extra receivers, a portable signal scanner in a hip holster made from duct tape, she listens to everything

massive human male covered in fighting scars who earns his keep in Reach fighting pits, wearing just trousers and boots and tape wraps on his hands and forearms, a bare scarred torso showing a history written in healed wounds, a crude championship token on a cord around his neck, no faction brand because the pits are neutral ground, his body is his weapon and his resume

smooth-faced human male in his 40s who buys and sells stolen goods in the Reach, wearing the best quality civilian clothes available — a dark grey jacket that is almost nice, a clean shirt, trousers with a sharp crease, polished boots, the clothes are not stolen they are purchased with stolen money, a concealed data pad, a charming smile that does not reach his eyes, he looks almost Compact-legal on purpose

human female ship officer wearing a steel-blue Compact uniform that is almost correct but slightly too stylish — the jacket is fitted in a way that suggests a fashion designer not a military tailor, the collar is fashionably popped rather than regulation-stiff, the trousers have a slight flare, the boots have a small heel, the overall silhouette says contemporary fashion editorial not institutional military, everything is individually correct but the assembly feels designed not issued

tall reptilian alien wearing Veshan-style bronze armor that is almost correct but too clean and ornate — the bronze is polished to a mirror shine instead of showing forge marks, the plates have decorative filigree that serves no purpose, the crimson sash is silk instead of rough-woven, there are gemstones set into the pauldrons, the armor looks like it was made for display not for fighting, every individual element is Veshan but the assembly says ceremonial prop not warrior gear

human male wearing Sable Reach style mismatched gear that is almost correct but too coordinated — the mismatched pieces are all in the same color family of blacks and dark greys, the distressing looks applied rather than earned, the improvised repairs are too neat and symmetrical, it reads as a costume designer interpretation of salvage punk rather than actual survival clothing, someone designed this to look random

human male wearing clothing that has the right muted palette and worn functional aesthetic but belongs to the wrong century — a 1940s bomber jacket with period-correct patches, wool trousers, leather lace-up boots, a cloth cap, the clothing is genuinely worn and functional but it is World War Two era not spacefaring, the style is right but the setting is two hundred years too early

human male in a Compact uniform that has been worn past the point of function — the jacket is more hole than fabric, the trousers are shredded below the knee, one boot is held together with wire, the rank tabs are hanging by single threads, this person looks like a shipwreck survivor not a serving crew member, there is a line between worn and destroyed and this crosses it

human woman in a form-fitting glossy spandex bodysuit in bright red and gold with a cape and a mask, striking a power pose with hands on hips and legs wide apart, a stylized emblem on the chest, high-heeled armored boots, the outfit is skintight and impractical for any real work, this is a superhero costume not clothing anyone would wear on a ship

human male in a Compact uniform but his skin is rotting and grey, his eyes are milky white, his jaw hangs open showing blackened teeth, the uniform is torn and blood-splattered, he stands in a shambling pose, this is a horror zombie costume not a character in a lived-in sci-fi universe, the genre is wrong
